title: Ordering at a HK bakery
description: Buy a pineapple bun and wife cake at a traditional Hong Kong bakery
context: Hong Kong bakeries (餅店) are a blend of Chinese and Western influences. Pineapple buns (菠蘿包) are arguably HK's most iconic pastry — despite the name, there's no pineapple in them. Wife cakes (老婆餅) are a sweet flaky pastry with winter melon filling.
culturalFact: The pineapple bun gets its name from the crinkled golden crust that looks like a pineapple skin. In 2014 it was granted 'intangible cultural heritage' status by the HK government — a serious honour for a bun.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, jiu3 loeng5 go3 bo1 lo4 baau1.
lines.english: Excuse me, I'd like two pineapple buns.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.jyutping: jiu3 m4 jiu3 gaa1 ngau4 jau4?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 要，加牛油。仲要兩件老婆餅。
lines.jyutping: jiu3, gaa1 ngau4 jau4. zung6 jiu3 loeng5 gin6 lou5 po4 beng2.
lines.english: Yes, with butter. And two wife cakes as well.

lines.speaker: staff
lines.cjk: 老婆餅係剛出爐㗎，好香㗎。
lines.jyutping: lou5 po4 beng2 hai6 gong1 ceot1 lou4 ga3, hou2 hoeng1 ga3.
lines.english: The wife cakes just came out of the oven, they smell great.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: hou2 je5! jat1 gung6 gei2 do1 cin2?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 唔該，呢係四十蚊，唔使找。
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ni1 hai6 sei3 sap6 man1, m4 sai2 zaau2.
lines.english: Here's forty dollars, keep the change.
